claims.claim: Andrej Karpathy announced he joined Anthropic's pretraining team on 2026-05-19, to build a team using Claude to accelerate pretraining research.
Nick Joseph (Anthropic Head of Pretraining) said Karpathy's team is focused on using Claude to accelerate pretraining research itself.
John Jumper left Google DeepMind for Anthropic on 2026-06-19 after nearly nine years, with no disclosed title, team, or mandate.

Anthropic's Claude Science product, launched 2026-06-30, credits Alexander Tarashansky and Eric Kauderer-Abrams as leads; neither MIT Technology Review's nor STAT News's launch coverage names Jumper as involved.
Ross Nordeen, an xAI co-founder who led its compute/infrastructure dealmaking, joined Anthropic in May 2026 to focus on compute.
Anthropic agreed to pay xAI roughly $1.25B/month for Colossus compute capacity through May 2029 (~$45B over three years).
Eric Boyd, former President of Microsoft's AI Platform / Azure AI lead, joined Anthropic as Head of Infrastructure on 2026-04-07.
Anthropic committed $100B+ over 10 years to AWS for up to 5GW of Trainium capacity, with roughly 1GW online by end of 2026.
Tom Blomfield, Monzo co-founder and YC group partner, took leave from YC to join Anthropic's compute team on 2026-07-13, working alongside co-founder Tom Brown.
Marcus Fontoura, former CTO of Microsoft Azure Core, joined Anthropic as Member of Technical Staff around 2026-06-17.
Jelani Nelson, chair of UC Berkeley's CS Division and a streaming-algorithms theorist, joined Anthropic's pretraining team on academic leave effective 2026-07-01.
Teresa Carlson (ex-AWS Worldwide Public Sector, Microsoft, Splunk) was named Anthropic's first-ever Global Head of Public Sector on 2026-07-07.
Anthropic's self-disclosed run-rate revenue rose from ~$9B (end 2025) to $14B (Feb 2026) to $30B (Apr 2026) to $47B (May 2026).
Anthropic closed a $65B Series H on 2026-05-28 at a $965B post-money valuation.
Anthropic confidentially submitted a draft Form S-1 to the SEC on 2026-06-01.
Anthropic's own Institute states: "We are not there yet, and recursive self-improvement is not inevitable."
Anthropic states more than 80% of code merged into its own codebase is Claude-authored, versus low single digits before Claude Code's Feb 2025 preview.
Boris Cherny (head of Claude Code) said he had not written a line of code by hand in roughly eight months as of June 2026.

Anthropic's government relationship was volatile in 2026: GSA OneGov deal (Aug 2025) -> Trump directive to cease use (Feb 27, 2026) -> DOD 'supply chain risk' designation (Mar 2026) -> federal lawsuit -> preliminary injunction restoring access (Mar 26, 2026) -> GSA reinstatement (Apr 3, 2026), with DOD-specific exclusion persisting after an Apr 8 appeals loss.
At least 22 professors left academia for frontier AI labs in H1 2026, split roughly evenly: OpenAI 6, Meta 6, Anthropic 5, Google DeepMind 5 — Anthropic is not the volume leader on this measure.
Google lost Noam Shazeer to OpenAI and John Jumper to Anthropic within 48 hours of each other (June 18-19, 2026), reported by press as one symmetrical talent-war event.

Three of the eight named 2026 hires (Karpathy, Boyd, Nordeen) joined Anthropic before the June 1 S-1 filing existed; five (Jumper, Fontoura, Nelson, Carlson, Blomfield) were announced 18-42 days after the filing.
SemiAnalysis reportedly estimates Anthropic's revenue per megawatt of compute capacity rose from ~$16M to a projected ~$60M within about nine months.
Dario Amodei's own estimate of algorithmic-efficiency progress is roughly 4x/year, up from a 2020 Anthropic-team estimate of 1.68x/year.
Anthropic's headcount is estimated between roughly 2,500 (Bloomberg, June 2026) and 5,189 (Tracxn, May 2026), with no official company disclosure.
